signal_title: Spain's Draft Organic Law for the Protection of Minors in Digital Environments
risk_rationale: The draft law introduces new requirements for parental controls and prohibits random reward mechanisms for minors, which are not currently addressed in TikTok's internal baseline. This represents a material compliance gap that requires immediate attention to align with potential new regulations in the EU market.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Parent visibility of teen social graph controls and other family pairing features.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current baseline provides optional parental controls through Family Pairing, but the draft law requires these controls to be included by default.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: No specific mention of random reward mechanisms for minors.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Prohibit random reward mechanisms for minors.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The baseline does not address the prohibition of random reward mechanisms for minors, which is a new requirement in the draft law.

remediations.title: Implement Default Parental Controls
remediations.description: Develop a feature that enables parental controls by default for all minor accounts in the EU region, in compliance with the draft law.
acceptance_criteria: Parental controls are enabled by default for all new minor accounts in the EU.
Existing minor accounts in the EU are prompted to enable parental controls.

remediations.title: Remove Random Reward Mechanisms for Minors
remediations.description: Identify and eliminate any random reward mechanisms available to minor users on the platform to comply with the draft law.
acceptance_criteria: All random reward mechanisms are disabled for minor accounts in the EU.
Compliance with the draft law is documented and verified.

business_impact: Failure to comply with the draft law could result in legal penalties and reputational damage in the EU market, potentially affecting user trust and platform growth.
recommended_actions: Conduct a detailed review of current parental control features.
Audit the platform for any existing random reward mechanisms.
Engage with EU regulators to clarify compliance expectations.
